original text:
Stay up-to-date with out latest news, partnerships, events and more.
Swissline’s Cell Shock White Brightening Diamond Serum has won the ultimate accolade from SpaChina, the leading wellness and luxury lifestyle magazine in China.
We are happy to announce our partnership with the breathtaking Buergenstock Hotels & Resort, perched in the mountains above Lake Lucerne in the heart of Switzerland.
After nearly three decades of helping women look younger and more vibrant, we decided that it was time to refresh our brand image.
We are proud to announce our strategic partnership with Clinic Lémanic, one of Switzerland’s top private clinics, a leader in the domain of anti-aging and aesthetic medicine.

original text:
The Port of Ipswich has recently handled a large shipment containing 5000 t of imported road salt, to be used by Suffolk Highways to help make local roads safer.
By transporting this road salt as far as possible by sea, ABP has helped minimise the impact on the local road network and has ensured that Suffolk is prepared for any further cold weather conditions.
ABP took the opportunity to invite colleagues from Suffolk County Council including cabinet member councillor Mary Evans and members of the Suffolk gritting team to see the salt cargo arriving at the port.
Mark Stevens, Suffolk Highways’ assistant director, said: “Despite the weather gradually getting warmer, it is important to be prepared for every eventuality. This salt delivery has topped up our stock so that we can both effectively treat the roads as and when required, and continue to complement each other’s winter maintenance service.
Paul Ager, ABP divisional port manager, East Anglia, said: “We were delighted to work closely with Suffolk Highways and Suffolk County Council to ensure that we have sufficient product to ensure the ongoing safety of roads in our county, whatever the weather.

original text:
The GasSuf exhibition will run from 22 till 24 October 2019 in Pavilions 4 at the Sokolniki ECC.
The cost of the ticket in booking office is 500 rubles.
Metro station “Sokolniki” (Sokolnicheskaya line, red 1), the only exits to from the station, further walk to the Sokolniki park.
The exhibition centre is located within the Sokolniki park territory which is in 10 minutes’ walk from the metro station.
The entry of cars is accessible through the 3rd Luchevoy Prosek. The cost of the entry by motocycle is 200 rubles; car is 500 rubles. You can leave your car at a specially equipped parking.
The cost of the entry by limousine, minibus, car with trailer are 700 rubles; buses, trucks, etc. are 1400 rubles; by multitran, tow truck, loader, crane are 6500 rubles.

original text:
How are suffering and joy connected? What is the purpose of suffering? How can God who is good allow us to suffer? How can we transcend suffering?
Our world view tends to dictate our response to suffering. No matter how we try to answer these questions one thing is for certain. We realize that suffering is a mystery. And this mystery has the power to transform us.
For persons of faith, we know that suffering can be turned into joy. Our greatest example is Jesus Christ who endured a horrific torture leading to death on a cross but was resurrected on the third day to bring salvation to the world. How can one not rejoice in that?
So one can say that the purpose of suffering is to bring us joy. One can also gain understanding, knowledge, wisdom, and hope through suffering. Suffering can increase virtues such as fortitude, humility, faith, hope, trust, etc. that would not be as easily attainable when one is not under a challenge and is in a state of complacency. In a perfect world there would be no need for suffering but since we don’t live in a perfect world, we enter into suffering to exit in a more perfect state.
Some ways to transcend suffering include “desert time” such as during the season of Lent. Depriving oneself of worldly pleasures, sacrificing and being of service to others, journeying with others in their sufferings, etc. — all of these help bring us to spiritual maturity and are of great benefit. Joining our sufferings to those of Christ on the Cross help us to see those sufferings in a new light with a renewed sense of purpose and hope.
So even though suffering is a mystery, we know that there is much to be gained through it. So rejoice and be glad for your sufferings.
I pray that your Lenten desert time has brought you to a new level of understanding, knowledge, wisdom, hope, and joy!
1. St. Francis of Assisi by Thomas of Celano, Franciscan Herald Press, Chicago 9, Illinois, 1963, p. 254.
2. The Little Flowers of St Francis, The “Fioretti”, edited by the Franciscan Father Zeffirino Lazzeri, Giulio Giannini & Son, Publishers, Florence, Italy, pp 44, 46.
You are currently browsing the Pearls of Wisdom Consulting Services blog archives for April, 2014.

original text:
Cronforce is an online project management software with timesheets and budget tracking tool. It can be utilized by freelancers and small companies as well as larger firms with smaller departments. Cronforce is a cloud-based software that can be used by project managers, HR managers, and finance managers. It offers modules, dashboards, and powerful analytics features to convert data into understandable graphics. Cronforce automates business processes to help manage resources, budgets, and time.
There are well-liked and widely used solutions in each software category. But are they automatically the best fit for your organization’s special wants? A market-leading software product may have thousands of customers, but does it provide what you need? For this reason, do not blindly shell out for popular systems. Read at least a few Cronforce reviews and consider the aspects that you wish to have in the software such as the cost, main features, available integrations etc. Then, select a few apps that fit your requirements. Try out the free trials of these platforms, read online comments, get explanations from the vendor, and do your investigation thoroughly. This in-depth groundwork is certain to help you select the finest software platform for your organization’s unique requirements.
How Much Does Cronforce Cost?
We realize that when you make a decision to buy Project Management Software it’s important not only to see how experts evaluate it in their reviews, but also to find out if the real people and companies that buy it are actually satisfied with the product. That’s why we’ve created our behavior-based Customer Satisfaction Algorithm™ that gathers customer reviews, comments and Cronforce reviews across a wide range of social media sites. The data is then presented in an easy to digest form showing how many people had positive and negative experience with Cronforce. With that information at hand you should be equipped to make an informed buying decision that you won’t regret.
What are Cronforce pricing details?
What integrations are available for Cronforce?
If you need more information about Cronforce or you simply have a question let us know what you need and we’ll forward your request directly to the vendor.

original text:
Teaching language to an artificial intelligence system is child’s play, thanks to MIT.
Researchers at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology developed a parser that imitates kids’ learning processes by observing the environment and making connections.
The system studies captioned videos and learns to associate words with objects and actions.
When given a new sentence, the parser can accurately predict its meaning—without visual clues.
This “weakly supervised” approach, which requires limited training input, could expand the types of data and reduce the effort needed for training parsers.
Annotations can certainly speed up the process, but they’re not needed to learn.
“People talk to each other in partial sentences, run-on thoughts, and jumbled language,” Barbu, a researcher in MIT’s McGovern Institute’s Center for Brains, Minds, and Machines (CBMM), said.
Ideally, an android equipped with MIT’s parser could constantly observe its environment, reinforcing its understanding of spoken commands—even when they aren’t fully grammatical or clear.
“You want a robot in your home that will adapt to their particular way of speaking … and still figure out what they mean,” he added.
The parser could also shed light on how young children learn language.
“A child has access to redundant, complementary information from different modalities, including hearing parents and siblings talk about the world, as well as tactile information and visual information, [which help him or her] to understand the world,” according to co-author Boris Katz, a principal research scientist and head of the CSAIL InfoLab Group.
The parser is described in a paper presented at this week’s Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing conference.
Moving forward, researchers are interested in modeling interactions, not just passive observations.
Would You Pay $10K For This AI-Generated Portrait?

original text:
Home » BLOOD PRESSURE GUIDE » Does Caffeine Raise Blood Pressure ?
If you suffer from Cardiovascular Disease (CVD or coronary heart disease) and/or fear you might some day due to high blood pressure, is your coffee consumption compounding the problem? Or, are there other things you can do to alleviate your high blood pressure and CVD concerns that do NOT include ending, or severely lessening, your coffee intake?
Can Coffee Contribute to Coronary Heart Disease?
A 1990 New England Journal of Medicine study of more than 45,000 men found no link between coffee, caffeine and CVD for those drinking four or more cups of coffee a day.These results confirm findings from the earlier Framingham Heart Study of more than 6,000 adults conducted over 20 years (as published in the Archives of Internal Medicine) and two 1987 studies using data from the Honolulu Heart Program (published in The New England Journal of Medicine and The American Journal of Epidemiology respectively).
Does Coffee/Caffeine Consumption Contribute to High Blood Pressure?
Despite previous controversy on the subject, most researchers now conclude that regular coffee and caffeine use has little or no effect on blood pressure.Studies reviewed in the Progress in Cardiovascular Diseases indicate that while first-time caffeine use may produce immediate, minimal changes in blood pressure, these changes are transient. No changes in blood pressure appear to occur in regular users of caffeine. A 1991 study published in the British Medical Journal reached the same conclusion and indicated that restricting caffeine did not reduce blood pressure in people with mild hypertension. A number of studies that have looked at people with normal blood pressure (published in the Archives of Internal Medicine and the American Journal of Nutrition) have concluded that caffeine does not contribute to hypertension.
I think it‘s safe to conclude that enough reliable evidence exists that can put to rest the claim that coffee/caffeine contributes to high blood pressure and even CVD.
What‘s the Better Alternative than Retiring Your Coffee Mug?
As a healthy conscious person, I‘ve learned that one of the most effective ways to counteract high blood pressure and the onset of CVD is to be physically active, NOT – as the studies above prove – eliminate caffeine consumption. In fact, caffeine can even help you become more fit. An American Heart Association study, as referenced by several peer-reviewed scientific and medical publications, has recently claimed that people who are physically active have a lower risk of getting high blood pressure — 20%-50% lower — than people who are not active.
Being physically active can reduce your risk for heart disease, help lower your total cholesterol level and raise HDL-cholesterol (the “good” cholesterol that does not build up in the arteries), and help lower high blood pressure.
And, again, people who are physically active have a lower risk of getting high blood pressure — 20 to 50 percent lower — than people who are not active.
More vigorous exercise has added benefits. It helps improve the fitness of the heart and lungs. And that in turn protects you more against heart disease and high blood pressure. Activities like swimming, brisk walking, running, and jumping rope are called “aerobic.” This means that the body uses oxygen to make the energy it needs for the activity. Aerobic activities can condition your heart and lungs if done at the right intensity for 30 minutes, three to four times a week. But if you don’t have 30 minutes for a break, try to find two 15-minute periods or even three 10-minute periods. Try to do some type of aerobic activity in the course of a week. And, since stress is also a major CAUSE of high blood pressure, exercise can even help lower stress levels as well.
Coffee consumption does not contribute to high blood pressure and CVD. Retiring your coffee mug is not going to help you reduce your risks. Exercising, however, will. So, go ahead and drink your coffee – especially before your next workout!
Most people don’t need to see a doctor before they start exercising, since a gradual, sensible exercise program has few health risks. But if you have a health problem like high blood pressure; if you have pains or pressure in the chest or shoulder area; if you tend to feel dizzy or faint; if you get very breathless after a mild workout; or are middle-age or older and have not been active, and you are planning a vigorous exercise program, you should check with your doctor first. Otherwise, drink your coffee, get out, get active, and get fit — and help prevent high blood pressure and CVD.
Why is High Blood Pressure so Dangerous ?

Otherwise, drink your coffee, get out, get active, and get fit — and help prevent high blood pressure and CVD.
Why is High Blood Pressure so Dangerous ?

original text:
Earlier this month Josh Frydenberg the Energy Minister wrote that the states’ renewable energy targets are leading to policy chaos. But in fact the opposite is true. It is the national policy chaos that Minister Frydenberg is presiding over that is leading states to step in with their own policies to support renewable investment.
Today in the Australian newspaper, the Minister for Environment and Energy, Josh Frydenberg, engaged in a grand exercise of attacking Labor policy. In doing so, he used all the usual tricks of a government desperate to distract from its own record; misrepresentation of Opposition policy, mischaracterisation of modelling and of course, a fanciful description of the actual challenges we face.
For a Government that has been so vocal about not picking winners, the Turnbull Government seems to be very comfortable picking coal as Australia’s future energy source.
This Centrelink debacle has to end, it has already caused too much unnecessary stress and anxiety. The Government needs to recognise the pain they have caused. I have written to Social Services Minister Alan Tudge urging him to immediately suspend the program.

original text:
Snooping in medicine cabinets used to be a lot more informing. (Uh, not that we’ve snooped.) Anyways, our point: A rising number of medicines are being used for reasons other than what it says on the label. Unlike prescription drug abuse of the past, this isn’t all about the latest party drugs. Today, people are lured toward medicines for unique psychological benefits—whether to perk up a dud personality or calm jittery nerves. While pill-poppers have gotten loopy on codeine-based medications for years, now it’s common for people to pop heart medicine to cure test anxiety or load up on narcolepsy meds to crank out a presentation.
Even the Food and Drug Administration can’t keep up with drug companies hawking the marketable side effects of their remedies. A new report from the Kaiser Institute shows it takes the FDA 7 months to issue a warning for this type of illegal marketing, and companies average 4 months to take action. As a result, more people are popping pills for ailments for which drugs weren’t tested and approved to treat—and patient safety is diminished. Before you get caught in a web of mixed medicines, consult this review of what’s showing up in medicine cabinets, what they can do to you, and alternatives that are side-effect free.
What does it do? Creates a euphoric sense similar to cocaine. Improves mood, fatigue, sleepiness and cognition like caffeine, but for a longer period of time. Helps promote wakefulness and alertness.
Who gets prescribed this? Patients with Attention Deficit Disorder (ADD), Attention Deficit Hyperactive Disorder (ADHD), and narcolepsy. Provigil can also help with obstructive sleep apnea and is prescribed to elderly patients who need help waking up in the morning.
Who abuses this? Stimulants are the performance-enhancing drugs of the corporate world. Wall Street professionals and lawyers are said to like Provigil because of its easy accessibility, adding to it’s appeal—this drug is less regulated than Ritalin and Adderall. Late-night partiers may also rely on these to get through the next day, says Dr. Tucker Woods, Chairman of the Department of Emergency Medicine at Long Island College Hospital of Brooklyn.
Addictive? Stimulants can be habit forming if patients rely on them for energy.
Alternative medicine: Because doctors recommend trying to minimize medications at all cost, stick with coffee. It tastes better, and your co-workers can’t complain about you juicing. Other alternatives include natural stimulants like Ginseng, available at most health stores, although its effectiveness depends on the amount of extract, the type of ginseng, and the company that makes it. The Panax (also called Asian or Chinese) variety seems to work the best.
What does it do? Used to calm nerves, prevent anxiety attacks, and as a sleep aid.
Who gets prescribed this? Insomniacs and patients with anxiety problems like obsessive compulsive disorder (OCD) or social phobias. It can also be used to relax the muscles in patients with cerebral palsy or multiple sclerosis.
Who abuses this? These drugs create a calming effect, and recreate the effects of feeling drunk. Those who abuse this will lapse further into a more relaxed state. Desired by thrill-seekers wanting to intensify a drunken night out, these types of drugs have an additive effect when combined with alcohol, amplifying the effects and possibly causing death. “Alcohol slows down your breathing and benzodiazepines slow down your breathing in a different way—the combine effect can kill you,” says Philip Wenger, PharmD, assistant professor at St. Louis College of Pharmacy.
Addictive? Yes, when used chronically.
Alternative medicine: If stress is what’s eating you, try to make a habit of mid-afternoon workouts. A study by researchers at the University of Missouri-Columbia found high-intensity exercise greatly reduces stress and anxiety. Making sleep a routine can also help: try to get to bed at the same time and wake up at the same time every night. The herbal supplement, melatonin, is sometimes used for sleep and research shows it can help you nod off—especially if you’re suffering from jet-lag.
What does it do? Antidepressants stimulate chemical changes that increase the levels of neurotransmitters associated with mood, including serotonin, norepinephrine and dopamine.
Who abuses this? Some use antidepressants as a quick-fix for moodiness, which critics say would be better addressed with a healthy diet and getting a full 8 hours of sleep.
Alternative medicine: Talk to a therapist and see if your depression is something you can work out through discussion, rather than meds. For over the counter stuff, St. John’s Wort is a supplement sometimes taken for depression, but be careful because it can interact with post-surgery medicine and some high blood pressure prescriptions —so check with your doctor first. Or you can eat your way to a good mood with grilled fish, like salmon. A study in Finland found that people who eat more fish are 31 percent less likely to suffer from depression because they contain feel-good omega-3 fatty acids.
What does it do? These sedatives relax the body by binding to neurotransmitters in the brain, producing a calming effect.
stimulants such as amphetamines (including methamphetamine), cocaine, and ecstasy. Some people take it also to get a high—they will fight the sleeping affects and try to stay awake because of the sensation it gives them, which include vivid visuals and a body high.
Addictive? This drug only takes about 15 minutes to kick in, so it can be habit-forming. Negative side affects can include hallucinations and forgetfulness.
Alternative medicine: Try a natural sleep aid, like melatonin, or an herbal supplement like Valerian. Or try some yoga poses before bed to stretch the body and relax the mind.
What does it do? This type of heart medication is used to treat high blood pressure and migraines. It’s been used to treat anxiety since the 70s. Beta blockers steady a shaking hand and slow a pounding heart by blocking adrenaline-like chemicals in the system, forcing the heart to beat slowly.
Who gets prescribed this? Used to treat patients with high blood pressure. Performers and musicians can also get a prescription to cure stage fright.
Who abuses this? Since the off-label use isn’t harmful, musicians, public speakers and surgeons use this to calm nerves and steady the hand.
Addictive? Beta blockers are non-habit forming.
processed_text:
Snooping in medicine cabinets used to be a lot more informing. (Uh, not that we've snooped.) Anyways, our point: A rising number of medicines are being used for reasons other than what it says on the label. Unlike prescription drug abuse of the past, this isn't all about the latest party drugs. Today, people are lured toward medicines for unique psychological benefits—whether to perk up a dud personality or calm jittery nerves. While pill-poppers have gotten loopy on codeine-based medications for years, now it's common for people to pop heart medicine to cure test anxiety or load up on narcolepsy meds to crank out a presentation.
Even the Food and Drug Administration can't keep up with drug companies hawking the marketable side effects of their remedies. A new report from the Kaiser Institute shows it takes the FDA 7 months to issue a warning for this type of illegal marketing, and companies average 4 months to take action. As a result, more people are popping pills for ailments for which drugs weren't tested and approved to treat—and patient safety is diminished. Before you get caught in a web of mixed medicines, consult this review of what's showing up in medicine cabinets, what they can do to you, and alternatives that are side-effect free.
What does it do? Creates a euphoric sense similar to cocaine. Improves mood, fatigue, sleepiness and cognition like caffeine, but for a longer period of time. Helps promote wakefulness and alertness.
Who gets prescribed this? Patients with Attention Deficit Disorder (ADD), Attention Deficit Hyperactive Disorder (ADHD), and narcolepsy. Provigil can also help with obstructive sleep apnea and is prescribed to elderly patients who need help waking up in the morning.
Who abuses this? Stimulants are the performance-enhancing drugs of the corporate world. Wall Street professionals and lawyers are said to like Provigil because of its easy accessibility, adding to it's appeal—this drug is less regulated than Ritalin and Adderall. Late-night partiers may also rely on these to get through the next day, says Dr. Tucker Woods, Chairman of the Department of Emergency Medicine at Long Island College Hospital of Brooklyn.
Addictive? Stimulants can be habit forming if patients rely on them for energy.
Alternative medicine: Because doctors recommend trying to minimize medications at all cost, stick with coffee. It tastes better, and your co-workers can't complain about you juicing. Other alternatives include natural stimulants like Ginseng, available at most health stores, although its effectiveness depends on the amount of extract, the type of ginseng, and the company that makes it. The Panax (also called Asian or Chinese) variety seems to work the best.
What does it do? Used to calm nerves, prevent anxiety attacks, and as a sleep aid.
Who gets prescribed this? Insomniacs and patients with anxiety problems like obsessive compulsive disorder (OCD) or social phobias. It can also be used to relax the muscles in patients with cerebral palsy or multiple sclerosis.
Who abuses this? These drugs create a calming effect, and recreate the effects of feeling drunk. Those who abuse this will lapse further into a more relaxed state. Desired by thrill-seekers wanting to intensify a drunken night out, these types of drugs have an additive effect when combined with alcohol, amplifying the effects and possibly causing death. "Alcohol slows down your breathing and benzodiazepines slow down your breathing in a different way—the combine effect can kill you," says Philip Wenger, PharmD, assistant professor at St. Louis College of Pharmacy.
Addictive? Yes, when used chronically.
Alternative medicine: If stress is what's eating you, try to make a habit of mid-afternoon workouts. A study by researchers at the University of Missouri-Columbia found high-intensity exercise greatly reduces stress and anxiety. Making sleep a routine can also help: try to get to bed at the same time and wake up at the same time every night. The herbal supplement, melatonin, is sometimes used for sleep and research shows it can help you nod off—especially if you're suffering from jet-lag.
What does it do? Antidepressants stimulate chemical changes that increase the levels of neurotransmitters associated with mood, including serotonin, norepinephrine and dopamine.
Who abuses this? Some use antidepressants as a quick-fix for moodiness, which critics say would be better addressed with a healthy diet and getting a full 8 hours of sleep.
Alternative medicine: Talk to a therapist and see if your depression is something you can work out through discussion, rather than meds. For over the counter stuff, St. John's Wort is a supplement sometimes taken for depression, but be careful because it can interact with post-surgery medicine and some high blood pressure prescriptions —so check with your doctor first. Or you can eat your way to a good mood with grilled fish, like salmon. A study in Finland found that people who eat more fish are 31 percent less likely to suffer from depression because they contain feel-good omega-3 fatty acids.
What does it do? These sedatives relax the body by binding to neurotransmitters in the brain, producing a calming effect.
stimulants such as amphetamines (including methamphetamine), cocaine, and ecstasy. Some people take it also to get a high—they will fight the sleeping affects and try to stay awake because of the sensation it gives them, which include vivid visuals and a body high.
